title: Human-notes status-vocabulary drift is silent
summary: Operators are writing human-notes with status values that the consuming agent silently ignores. Two recent examples: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001/002 were written with status="unclaimed". Analyst preflight (analyst.md line ~105) filters on status==="pending" only. The notes sat dormant until the operator noticed by chance. There is no schema enforcement, no warning, no diagnostic in any agent run — it is silent failure. Tinker: propose a schema + enforcement strategy.

context.observed_pattern: Three distinct statuses live in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json today: "consumed" (processed by agent), "pending" (triggers action), "unclaimed" (operator meant "pending" but agent ignores). Same pattern is likely latent in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json, monitor/social/human-notes.json, monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json — Tinker should audit all of them.
context.root_cause: No schema file, no validator, no agent-side strictness. Operators (human and Claude-operator) fall back to intuitive English words like "unclaimed", "new", "todo" that do not appear in the prompt preflights.
context.impact: Direct: the design-refresh Phase A EXP-202 revision cycle was delayed by roughly one hour while the operator identified the flipped bit and re-pushed. Systemic: every HNOTE written with a non-canonical status is effectively a no-op, and this is invisible in agent reports — the note simply never shows up in the "pending notes" count.
context.specific_incident: On 2026-04-22, HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001 (self-host fonts) and -002 (grandfather GA under revised NC8, fix EXP-202 c_4) were written with status="unclaimed". Analyst was scheduled to pick them up at 03:57Z UTC; preflight would have reported "NO NOTES". Operator flipped both to "pending" via commit cb540b1 before the run fired.

proposed_fix_spec.deliverable: Propose a schema + enforcement strategy. Specific choice is yours; the pattern to optimize for is "operator writes a sensible-looking note, if they use a wrong word it fails loudly, not silently."

options_to_consider.option: A — JSON Schema file + validator step
options_to_consider.description: Add monitor/prompts/reference/human-notes.schema.json with status enum ["pending","consumed","deferred","rejected"]. Add a validation step to either workspace-sync, integrity, or a new pre-commit hook in build.js that fails loudly if any status is out of enum.
options_to_consider.tradeoff: Most rigorous; requires wiring validation into something that runs frequently. Integrity agent runs daily, which may be too slow.

options_to_consider.description: Change analyst.md / decider.md / etc. preflights from status==="pending" to status!=="consumed"&&status!=="deferred"&&status!=="rejected". Any other value (including "unclaimed") triggers action.
options_to_consider.tradeoff: Forgiving. But accepts genuine typos ("pendng") as also valid, which has its own failure mode.

options_to_consider.description: Notes without a status field default to triggering. Consuming an note sets status="consumed". Simpler mental model (the only state is consumed-or-not).
options_to_consider.tradeoff: Loses expressiveness — no way to say "deferred until X" or "rejected, keep for audit".

options_to_consider.description: Schema defines the canonical set, AND preflights widen to accept "any non-consumed" as pending. Schema violations get logged (not blocked) so the loudness is there without the fragility.
options_to_consider.tradeoff: Two layers of redundancy. Probably right, but the most code to write.

thinking_prompts: How many HNOTE files total — one per agent, or shared? (monitor/*/human-notes.json appears to be a per-agent convention.)
Are there any existing statuses in use that are NOT pending/consumed? Audit all four files before proposing enum.
Should the schema cover other fields (target_agent, priority, body presence)? Err on the side of minimum viable enforcement; schema scope creep has its own cost.
Where should the validator run? Workspace-sync (every 4h), integrity (daily 9am), or build.js pre-commit (on every decider push)?

deliverable_requested.format: PROP file in monitor/tinker/proposals/ following existing PROP-* naming conventions.
contents: Audit: enumerate every distinct status value found across all four human-notes.json files, with counts per agent. This is 5 minutes of grep — must be done before proposing.
Recommended schema (minimum: status enum; optional: required fields, valid target_agent values)
Enforcement mechanism chosen from A/B/C/D above (or new option) with justification
Exact patch text for any prompt files or build.js that need changes
Migration plan: what to do with existing notes that have non-canonical statuses at apply time
Rollback plan if enforcement blocks a legitimate note the operator needs processed urgently

non_goals: Do NOT propose replacing the human-notes convention entirely — it works, this is a sharpening pass.
Do NOT propose merging the four human-notes.json files into one. Per-agent separation is a deliberate access-control boundary.
Do NOT add runtime-side note classification (e.g. tag detection). Keep the vocabulary small and human-legible.
completion_criteria: Tinker produces a PROP file addressing the deliverable list above. Operator reviews + approves specific enforcement choice before any prompt or build.js patch is applied.
superseded_reason: Scope widened. The underlying issue (silent-ignore of operator-surfaced state) generalizes beyond human-notes to the cross-agent complaint audit addressed in 002. Treat this directive as the first example case within 002, not as a standalone task.
